revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Aburahi-jinja]][[ja:油日神社]] {{Expand Japanese|油日神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Aburahi Shrine | native_name = 油日神社 | image = Aburahi-jinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Aburahi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.887027777777774|136.24972222222223}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Aburahi no Ōkami|lt=Aburahi no Ōkami|qid=Q137194160|1=Aburahi no Ōkami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Aburahi Shrine'''|油日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|lt=kokushi genzaisha|en|Kokushi genzaisha|fr|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|simple|Kokushi genzaisha|qid=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province|ja|近江国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11638477|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 21 ** P155: {{ill|Ihimichino Shrine|lt=Ihimichino Shrine|qid=Q135039797|1=Ihimichino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Mikami Shrine|lt=Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|ja|御上神社|qid=Q11488762|1=Mikami Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|lt=Kōka-shi|ar|كوكا (شيغا)|azb|کوکا، شیقا|ceb|Kōka-shi|ce|Кока (гӀала)|de|Kōka|en|Kōka, Shiga|envoyage|Koka|es|Kōka (Shiga)|fa|کوکا، شیگا|fi|Kōka|fr|Kōka (Shiga)|gl|Kōka, Shiga|hy|Կոկա (քաղաք)|id|Kōka, Shiga|it|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|javoyage|甲賀市|ko|고카시|mzn|کوکا، شیگا|nl|Koka (Shiga)|pl|Kōka|pt|Kōka (Shiga)|ro|Kōka|ru|Кока (город)|sv|Kōka, Shiga|tg|Кока|th|โคกะ (จังหวัดชิงะ)|tl|Kōka, Shiga|tr|Kōka, Shiga|tt|Кока (шәһәр)|uk|Кока (Сіґа)|ur|کوکا، شیگا|vi|Kōka, Shiga|war|Kōka, Shiga|zh_classical|甲賀市|zh_min_nan|Kôka-chhī|zh_yue|甲賀|zh|甲賀市|qid=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}} * {{ill|Ōmi Province|lt=Ōmi Province|ca|Província d'Ōmi|ckb|پارێزگای ئۆمی|cs|Provincie Ómi|de|Provinz Ōmi|en|Ōmi Province|es|Provincia de Ōmi|fa|ولایت اومی|fi|Ōmin provinssi|fr|Province d'Ōmi|gl|Provincia de Ōmi|hak|Ōmi-koet|id|Provinsi Ōmi|it|Ōmi|ja|近江国|ko|오미국|lt|Omio žemė|nl|Omi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Ōmi|pt|Província de Ōmi|ru|Оми (провинция)|simple|Ōmi Province|sk|Ómi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Оми|tr|Ōmi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Омі|vi|Tỉnh Ōmi|zh_min_nan|Ômi-kok|zh_yue|近江國|zh|近江國|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} * {{ill|Kōka District, Shiga|lt=Kōka district|en|Kōka District, Shiga|fa|بخش کوکا، شیگا|ja|甲賀郡|ru|Кока (уезд)|tr|Kōka İlçesi, Shiga|zh|甲賀郡|qid=Q4227244|1=Kōka District, Shiga}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.887027777777774|136.24972222222223}} == image (P18) == * Aburahi-jinja.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Aburahi no Ōkami|lt=Aburahi no Ōkami|qid=Q137194160|1=Aburahi no Ōkami}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|pl|Mizuhanome|sv|Mizuhanome|zh|彌都波能賣神|qid=Q8191796|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|az|Sarutahiko|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|fa|ساروتاهیکو اوکامی|fr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|it|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|サルタヒコ|ko|사루타히코 오카미|mni|ꯁꯔꯨꯇꯍꯤꯀꯣ ꯑꯣꯀꯃꯤ|pl|Sarutahiko|ru|Сарутахико|simple|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ta|சருதாகிகோ அகாமி|tr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|zh|猿田彥|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181801 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|ko|정5위|zh|正五位|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あぶらひじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 256246478 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7160005002853 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00647583 == Commons category (P373) == * Aburahi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahakareno Shrine|lt=Kahakareno Shrine|qid=Q135039798|1=Kahakareno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日1042', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Aburahi-jinja}} [[JA:油日神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11554359}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Aburahi Shrine in Aburahi, Kōka Town, Kōka City, Shiga Prefecture|A shrine of the same name in Kamiumasugi, Kōnan Town, Kōka City|Aburahi Shrine (Kamiumasugi, Kōnan Town, Kōka City)}} {{Shrine |Name = Aburahi Shrine |Image = [[Image:Aburahi-jinja.JPG|270px]]
Rōmon gate |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka City|Q847584|qid=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}{{ill|lt=Aburahi, Kōka Town|Q86737182|qid=Q86737182|1=Aburahi, Kōka Town}}1042 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Aburahi Ōkami |Shrine rank = Shrine listed in the national chronicles; formerly a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}; debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yōmei|Q349281|qid=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (or {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) |Main hall = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|qid=Q2544|1=May 1}} |Rituals = Taiko dance (May 1) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Aburahi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Aburahi Shrine''' (あぶらひじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka City|Q847584|qid=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Aburahi, Kōka Town|Q86737182|qid=Q86737182|1=Aburahi, Kōka Town}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shrine recorded in the national chronicles|Q118304363|qid=Q118304363|1=Kokushi genzaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. The enshrined deity, Aburahi Ōkami, was revered by {{ill|lt=samurai|Q1975935|qid=Q1975935|1=bushi}} as a god of victory, and from the shrine’s name was also worshipped as a god of the fire of oil. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Aburahi|1=UNKNOWN}} is regarded as the {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}, and at its summit stands Take Shrine, which enshrines the goddess Mizuhanome. The shrine crest is a {{ill|lt=mokko|Q3894703|qid=Q3894703|1=Papaya (disambiguation)}} combined with a {{ill|lt=hikiryō mon|Q11487136|qid=Q11487136|lt=double-bar design|1=Hikiryō mon}}. === History === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, but it is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yōmei|Q349281|qid=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}} or {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. According to legend, the name “Aburahi” derives from the descent of the Aburahi deity upon Mount Aburahi accompanied by a light like the flame of oil. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}} built the shrine buildings and enshrined Aburahi Daimyōjin. The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=national chronicles|1=Rikkokushi}} is in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, under the entry for {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1st year, {{ill|lt=12th month, 3rd day (lunar calendar)|Q841033|qid=Q841033|lt=December 3|1=December 3 (Lunisolar)}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|qid=Q23910|1=878}} {{ill|lt=January 9|Q2238|qid=Q2238|1=January 9}}), which states: “Aburahi no Kami of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, ranked Senior Sixth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fifth Rank Lower ({{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}).” There is a theory that identifies it with the “Kawakare Shrine, two seats” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Kōga District|Q4227244|qid=Q4227244|1=Kōka District, Shiga}},*Ōmi Meiseki Annaiki* but this is difficult to accept,Uno, “Kawakare Shrine.” The same *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku* records under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3rd year, {{ill|lt=4th month, 8th day (lunar calendar)|Q839007|qid=Q839007|lt=April 8|1=April 8 (Lunisolar)}} ({{ill|lt=861|Q23913|qid=Q23913|1=861}} {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|qid=Q2580|1=May 21}}): “Kawakare no Kami of Ōmi Province, Junior Fifth Rank Lower, is granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower.” Since by {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|qid=Q23905|1=877}}) it was already Senior Fifth Rank Lower, the accounts do not align. In the main hall there is a ridge tag dated {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|qid=Q6687|1=1493}}) bearing the inscription “Shōichi-i Aburahi Daimyōjin,” which clarifies the construction period. It was the foremost renowned shrine in the Kōga region, and in the medieval period the {{ill|lt=Kōga-ryū|Q6455112|qid=Q6455112|lt=Kōga warriors|1=Kōga-ryū}} revered {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}} as a war god and worshipped it as the “{{ill|lt=general shrine|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of Kōga.” For a long time, {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (the syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism) was practiced, and Aburahi-ji (Kongō-ji) served as the shrine-temple. However, in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, with the implementation of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the shrine was separated from Aburahi-ji, and Aburahi-ji changed its name to Jingū-ji and became independent. Nevertheless, many Buddhist-related cultural properties remain at the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1906|Q2049|qid=Q2049|1=1906}} (Meiji 39), the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}, and in {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), ten shrines within the village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|lt=enshrined together|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} as subordinate shrines. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – Aburahi Ōkami * East side hall – {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796}} * West side hall – {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} === Precincts === * Main hall ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1493|Q6687|qid=Q6687|1=1493}}). Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. * Inner gate * Worship hall (Important Cultural Property) – Rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}. {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, gable entrance, cypress bark roofing. * East cloister (Important Cultural Property) – Built in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1566|Q6638|qid=Q6638|1=1566}}). Cypress bark roofing. * West cloister (Important Cultural Property) – Built in Eiroku 9 (1566). Cypress bark roofing. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) – Built in Eiroku 9 (1566). Irimoya-zukuri, cypress bark roofing. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|qid=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|qid=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|Q11505065|qid=Q11505065|1=Hiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|qid=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Sakura Shrine * Tsunematsu Shrine * Ancestral Spirit Shrine * Former sutra repository * Kōga Historical and Folklore Museum * Bell tower – The hanging temple bell is designated a tangible cultural property of Kōka City. It was cast in {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1620|Q6810|qid=Q6810|1=1620}}) by founders from {{ill|lt=Kurita District|Q6446084|qid=Q6446084|1=Kurita District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Tsujimura|Q11638251|qid=Q11638251|1=Tsuji}}, through donations by {{ill|lt=Yamaoka Kageyori|Q11467812|qid=Q11467812|1=Yamaoka Kageyori}} and others. It is a remnant of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. * Shrine office * Assembly hall * Off-site shrines ** Take Shrine ** Shirahige Shrine File:Aburahi Shrine - wooden torii.jpg|Wooden torii gate File:Aburahi Shrine - purification pavilion.jpg|Purification pavilion File:Aburahi Shrine - worship hall.jpg|Worship hall File:Aburahi Shrine - main hall.jpg|Main hall File:Cloister at Aburahi jinja in Koka.jpg|Cloister File:Bell tower at Aburahi jinja in Koka 02.jpg|Bell tower === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Main hall, with: 15 ridge tags * Worship hall * Rōmon gate and cloisters, 3 structures, with: 3 ridge tags === National Historic Site === * Aburahi Shrine precincts (part of the {{ill|lt=Kōga District Nakasō|Q85882491|qid=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}} ruins group) === Nationally Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Aburahi Taiko Dance (Aburahi Shrine Yakko-furi Taiko Dance Preservation Society) === Shiga Prefecture–Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Fukutayū deity mask, 1 piece, with: Zuzuiko doll, 1 figure === Shiga Prefecture–Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Taiko dance (Aburahi Shrine Taiko Dance Preservation Society) * {{ill|lt=Yakko-furi|Q11446618|qid=Q11446618|lt=Yakko procession|1=Yakko-furi}} (Aburahi Shrine Yakko-furi Preservation Society) === Kōka City–Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Hanging Buddhist image group of Aburahi Shrine * Three Thousand Seed Buddhas * Colored silk painting of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}} illustrated biography * Colored silk painting of the Eleven-Deity Mandala * Colored silk painting of the {{ill|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|Q10904910|qid=Q10904910|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}} triad mandala * Colored silk painting of the {{ill|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|qid=Q818468|1=Benzaiten}} triad mandala * Colored silk painting of the {{ill|lt=Twelve Devas|Q9503981|qid=Q9503981|1=Jūniten}} * Old masks * Temple bell === Kōka City–Designated Natural Monument === * {{ill|lt=Kōyamaki (Japanese umbrella pine)|Q161648|qid=Q161648|1=Sciadopitys verticillata}} === Festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aburahi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}), {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|qid=Q2544|1=May 1}} : The taiko dance is a nationally selected intangible folk cultural property, and the {{ill|lt=Yakko-furi|Q11446618|qid=Q11446618|1=Yakko-furi}} is a prefecturally selected intangible folk cultural property. * Mi-umare Festival === References === * Uno Shigeki, “Kawakare Shrine” (in *Survey Report on Shikinaisha*, Vol. 12, Tōsandō 1, {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press}}, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 56 ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}})) === Notes === {{Help for footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat| Aburahi-jinja}} * Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Shiga Prefecture}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated
